critic_raw: The design trains 'cross-entropy loss without class weighting unless class imbalance is severe.' For a 300,000-500,000 ticket dataset with typical escalation rates of 5-20%, class imbalance is moderate (5:1 to 20:1 ratio). Whether to apply class weighting is a design decision that affects the model's recall-precision tradeoff. If class weighting is not applied and the model is trained on 5:1 imbalance, the model may favor precision over recall — which could make the PR-AUC comparison unfair if TF-IDF receives different weighting.
defender_raw: The design specifies that both models would receive the same class weighting treatment: 'Training uses cross-entropy loss without class weighting unless class imbalance is severe (checked during initial exploration).' The TF-IDF+LR baseline's class weighting is also specified: 'Logistic regression is trained on TF-IDF features with L2 regularization' — class weighting for LR would also be applied consistently. Both models receive equivalent treatment of class imbalance. If class weighting is applied to one, it would be applied to both. PR-AUC integrates across all thresholds and is less sensitive to the choice of class weighting than accuracy or F1 — both models' full PR curves are compared.
adjudication_raw: One concern: class weighting decision affects recall-precision tradeoff for both models. Addressed: both models receive same treatment; PR-AUC integrates across all thresholds and is robust to class weighting choice. Verdict: defense_wins.
